form.label: Reliability Consistency & Predictability
form.description: Evaluates how consistent and predictable service reliability is over time. Highlights noisy services, behavioural variance, and reliability volatility using log-derived failure signals only.

table.title: Services with the highest variance in failure behavior. These services generate operational noise and increase incident risk.

search.query: `svc_failure_detect($index$)` | timechart span=1h sum(is_failure) AS failures BY service | untable _time service failures | stats stdev(failures) AS failure_variance avg(failures) AS avg_failures BY service | eval predictability = case( failure_variance >= 5, "LOW", failure_variance >= 2, "MEDIUM", true(), "HIGH" ) | sort - failure_variance

chart.title: Distribution of failure variability across services. Long tails indicate services with highly erratic behavior.

search.query: `svc_failure_detect($index$)` | timechart span=1h sum(is_failure) AS failures BY service | untable _time service failures | stats stdev(failures) AS failure_variance BY service

chart.title: Tracks changes in failure variability over time. Rising volatility indicates worsening predictability and higher incident risk.

div.p: This dashboard evaluates how predictable and consistent service reliability behavior is over time. It complements availability and SLA views by highlighting operational noise and volatility.

ul.li: Consistency Score – Higher scores indicate stable, predictable failure behavior.
Failure Variance – Measures how much failure counts fluctuate over time.
Volatility Trend – Rising trends indicate increasing unpredictability.
High availability with low consistency indicates hidden operational risk.
Low consistency services should be reviewed before major releases.
Improving consistency over time indicates successful stabilization efforts.
Investigate services with high variance and low predictability.
Review retry logic, dependency behavior, and error handling.
